chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P.No.D- 59 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For Katcha Peshi. 3. For hearing of MA 12289/2017. 4. For hearing of MA 12894/2017. 11.10.2017. Applicant Syed Wahid Ali Shah present in person. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional A.G. = 1to3. Deferred. 4. The listed application has been filed by Inspector Wahid Ali Shah, seeking replacement of his assignment as a member of Girls Rescue Escort formed by the DIG Hyderabad in compliance of Para No.8 of the order dated 26.09.2017 in the instant petition on the grounds that he is suffering from kidney problem and presently is seized with holding of some official inquiries. According to him, DIG Hyderabad has constituted a Girls Rescue Centre / Escort for the safety of girls of Sindh University nominating him as a member of said Escort. And although the SSP Hyderabad under whom he is working has written a letter to the DIG Hyderabad Range for his replacement from the said assignment and has proposed the name of Inspector Parvez Ahmed Junejo at his place, but since his name is mentioned in Para No.8 of the above order, the DIG could not act upon the said letter and order for his replacement. Learned A.A.G. has no objection if the request of the applicant is allowed. We have seen Para No.8 of the above said order. The DIG Hyderabad has been directed to depute, amongst others, the applicant in the police Escort to be established for girls’ rescue in Sindh University. But since the applicant himself has shown his unwillingness on account of his health problems on the one hand and on the other has claimed to be pre-occupied in some official inquiries, we see no reason to order him to render his services at any cost because his services cannot be forcibly consigned to the Girls Rescue Escort and doing so would frustrate the very purpose for which this Rescue Escort has been ordered to be established.

The DIG Hyderabad has been directed to depute, amongst others, the applicant in the police Escort to be established for girls’ rescue in Sindh University. But since the applicant himself has shown his unwillingness on account of his health problems on the one hand and on the other has claimed to be pre-occupied in some official inquiries, we see no reason to order him to render his services at any cost because his services cannot be forcibly consigned to the Girls Rescue Escort and doing so would frustrate the very purpose for which this Rescue Escort has been ordered to be established. Accordingly, let the DIG Hyderabad consider the replacement of the applicant as per terms of letter of SSP dated 05.10.2017 without being influenced by the mentioning of name of the applicant in Para No.8 of the referred order. The application stands disposed of in above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Tufail
